author: Susan "Lady D" Damon
release_boundary: Line-edited pilot batch for review. This is not final KDP upload approval.

source_files.library_source: /Users/IDC2.5/Documents/LADY D/Production Library/01 Surrendering to God's Love/01 Manuscript/Month 01 - January/Days 001-007 Pilot Manuscript.md

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Awaken the Father's Welcome into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Begin this morning received, not measured; let the Father's welcome steady your first step.
edits.reason: Breaks the repeated impact frame and speaks directly to the entry's welcome-versus-shame theme.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Embrace Beloved Identity into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Walk into today from belovedness; no fear gets to write your name before the Father does.
edits.reason: Makes identity concrete and memorable without flattering the reader.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Remember Love That Finds You into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Let one ordinary task become worship, and let the Father's steadiness meet you there.
edits.reason: Ties the impact line to the day's ordinary-action-as-worship application.
Aligns the title with the grace/striving language used by the proof decision lane.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Walk in Grace Before Striving into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Lay down one mask today and let grace tell the truth gently before you strive.
edits.reason: Turns the repeated impact template into an action matched to the day's performance theme.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Let Peace Lead Mercy in the Morning into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Let peace make room for mercy; notice one person the Father's love is asking you to see.
edits.reason: Makes the mercy application observable and reader-actionable.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Choose the Father's Patience into one faithful step today.
edits.after: When the timeline feels tight, choose one patient breath and cling to the Father before control reaches for the wheel.
edits.reason: Names the tension of waiting and preserves the `dabaq` cling-to-God lens.

edits.before: This is also an Adventist-safe Sabbath guardrail for the whole devotional system: obedience is not presented as earning love. True obedience grows from a heart renewed by God. The seventh-day Sabbath, and every act of faithful living, should be received as a gift-shaped response to the Creator and Redeemer, not as a badge of spiritual superiority.
edits.after: For Adventist readers, this matters deeply: obedience is not presented as a way to earn love. True obedience grows from a heart renewed by God. The seventh-day Sabbath, and every act of faithful living, can be received as a gift-shaped response to the Creator and Redeemer, not as a badge of spiritual superiority.
edits.reason: Removes internal production wording while preserving the Adventist Sabbath and grace guardrail.

edits.before: Let the Father's love carry Listen for Love Stronger Than Fear into one faithful step today.
edits.after: Open the guarded place to the Father; obedience can grow from renewed love, not fear.
edits.reason: Connects inward heart renewal to grace-shaped obedience.

judge_auditor_loops.evidence: Seven impact lines now vary by entry theme and avoid the repeated `Let the Father's love carry` frame.
Day 007 keeps Saturday Sabbath and obedience-as-response-to-grace wording while removing internal production phrasing.
The pilot file now has zero `Let the Father's love carry` morning-impact lines.
Source, public mirror, and Production Library source all receive the same edited manuscript.

next_loop: Continue author-voice line edits in seven-day batches, then regenerate masters/interiors and update the review site after each meaningful completed gate.
